title: nie-daemon server core: AppState, CORS, token auth, PID file
description:
**What:** Implement DaemonState (Arc-shared server state holding identity, relay channels, directory, event broadcaster), token auth middleware, CORS (localhost-only), loopback binding validation, and PID file single-instance enforcement.

**Algorithm:**
1. Add deps to daemon/Cargo.toml: subtle='2', tokio (sync feature for broadcast), chrono.workspace, rand='0.8' (for token gen), uuid.workspace
2. Create daemon/src/state.rs:
   - struct Inner { my_pub_id: String, token: String, relay_tx: tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<nie_core::JsonRpcRequest>, directory: tokio::sync::Mutex<DirectoryState>, events_tx: tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender<DaemonEvent>, display_name: Option<String> }
   - struct DirectoryState { online: Vec<UserInfo>, offline: Vec<UserInfo> }
   - struct DaemonState(Arc<Inner>); impl Clone for DaemonState
   - impl DaemonState: fn my_pub_id() -> &str, fn token() -> &str, fn relay_tx() -> &mpsc::Sender<_>, fn broadcast_event(&self, e: DaemonEvent), async fn update_directory(&self, online: Vec<UserInfo>, offline: Vec<UserInfo>), async fn directory_snapshot(&self) -> DirectoryState
3. Create daemon/src/token.rs:
   - fn load_or_create_token(data_dir: &Path) -> Result<String>: read daemon.token if exists (mode check <= 0o600); else generate 32 random bytes → base64url → write with mode 0o600 using OpenOptionsExt::mode; return token string
   - fn validate_token_header(header_value: &str, expected: &str) -> bool: strip 'Bearer ' prefix; constant-time compare using subtle::ConstantTimeEq on byte slices; return false on any format error (never propagate details)
4. Create daemon/src/pid.rs:
   - fn acquire_pid_file(path: &Path) -> Result<()>: if path exists, read pid (u32), test if alive via kill(pid, 0); if alive return Err('daemon already running as PID {}'); if dead log warn and remove file; write current pid with mode 0o600
   - fn release_pid_file(path: &Path): remove file, log if fails (don't panic)
5. In daemon/src/main.rs: add LISTEN_ADDR env var (default 127.0.0.1:7734); parse as SocketAddr; assert ip.is_loopback() else bail!('LISTEN_ADDR must be loopback'); add tower_http::cors::CorsLayer allowing origins [http://localhost:7734, http://127.0.0.1:7734]; install auth extractor as axum middleware; acquire PID file before binding; release PID on tokio::signal::ctrl_c()

**Defensive programming rules:**
- Token comparison MUST use subtle::ConstantTimeEq — never string == comparison
- Token file created with mode 0o600 (OpenOptionsExt on Unix); verify on read
- Never log token value (not even truncated)
- LISTEN_ADDR non-loopback → bail!, never silently accept
- PID file written AFTER listener bind succeeds (not before)
- auth middleware returns 401 with generic JSON body — never reveals token hints

**Agent team:** Researcher (reads relay/src/main.rs for axum setup pattern) + Implementer + Validator.

**Tests (oracle: HTTP status codes are defined by RFC 7235, UUID format is 8-4-4-4-12 hyphenated):**
- test_loopback_validation: build a validation function, pass '0.0.0.0:0', verify Err returned
- test_validate_token_missing: call validate_token_header('', expected) → false
- test_validate_token_wrong: call validate_token_header('Bearer wrong', 'correct') → false
- test_validate_token_ok: call validate_token_header('Bearer correct', 'correct') → true
- test_stale_pid_cleaned: write PID of dead process to temp file, call acquire_pid_file, verify file overwritten with current pid
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-daemon passes; token auth rejection tests pass; cargo clippy -- -D warnings exits 0
close_reason: AppState, token auth, PID file implemented; tests pass
